behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is diarization/label fidelity from a real speech model; mocked STT can hide missing or unstable speaker labels. The behavior is not just local parsing logic, and correctness depends on real model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio with known speaker turns. Run transcription with the production STT provider, then run summarization. Assert speaker labels are present in transcript artifacts and that sampled attributed statements in the structured summary map to the expected attendees.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is extraction/attribution quality from LLM output. A deterministic mock would likely always pass and miss real misses/omissions in assignment detection.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript fixture with explicit task assignments per attendee, invoke the real summarization model, and assert each attendee with an assigned task appears as an assignee in at least one action item in the structured output.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is LLM content structuring: extracting decisions and placing them in the correct schema field. Mocked summarizers hide this placement/quality failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use a transcript containing clearly worded decisions plus general discussion. Call the real summarization model and assert expected decisions are present in the structured `decisions` field and not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff/state orchestration (chunking -> sequential processing -> merge). This can be caught with wired local components and controlled doubles; real model quality is secondary for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the orchestration with a >60-minute fixture (or synthetic metadata) and deterministic transcribe/summarize doubles that include segment IDs. Assert segment boundaries are created correctly, processing is strictly sequential, no segment is skipped/duplicated, and merged output is a single structured summary containing all segment contributions once.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires end-to-end success across multiple real agentic steps (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery. Single-step agentic or lower-level tests can miss pipeline interaction failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting recording through the production entrypoint. Use real STT and LLM services, wait for completion, and assert successful terminal state, valid structured schema, and automatic delivery to the configured output target with no manual retries or edits.
